commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=12e443454068b4fb768c53c4bcb23f473b7c680e)
commands.review_summary:
The PR changes Matrix raw transport reads without `maxBytes` to use the existing 64 MiB response cap and adds mocked plus `node:http` transport tests.

PR surface: Source -3, Tests +124. Total +121 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source inspection gives a high-confidence path: current main routes raw Matrix requests without `maxBytes` to `response.arrayBuffer()`, and encrypted media options are dropped at the MatrixClient crypto callback.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Human follow-up is needed because the external PR needs contributor real-behavior proof plus a compatibility-sensitive fix to the encrypted-media option path before merge.

target.title: fix(matrix): bound raw transport response reads to prevent OOM
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes unbounded raw body buffering in Matrix SDK transport when `maxBytes` is not provided by the caller.

**Root cause:** `performMatrixRequest` with `raw: true` falls through to `Buffer.from(await response.arrayBuffer())` when `params.maxBytes` is undefined. Any caller that omits `maxBytes` (e.g. `downloadContent(mxcUrl)` in `sdk.ts:532`) can buffer arbitrarily large media files — images, videos, encrypted attachments — exhausting process memory.

**Fix:** Use the existing `MATRIX_SDK_RESPONSE_MAX_BYTES` constant (already defined in the file) as a safe default upper bound. Remove the conditional split; all raw reads now go through `readResponseWithLimit`.

**Compatibility:** Callers that already pass `maxBytes` see no behavior change. Callers that omit it (previously unbounded) now get a `MatrixMediaSizeLimitError` if the response exceeds `MATRIX_SDK_RESPONSE_MAX_BYTES`. This is fail-closed: the alternative is unbounded OOM.

## Validation Evidence

Local terminal output (`node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run extensions/matrix/src/matrix/sdk/transport.test.ts --reporter=verbose`):

```
 RUN  v4.1.8 /media/vdc/.../openclaw

 ✓ performMatrixRequest > rejects oversized raw responses before buffering the whole body 40ms
 ✓ performMatrixRequest > rejects malformed raw content-length before buffering the body 4ms
 ✓ performMatrixRequest > applies streaming byte limits when raw responses omit content-length 14ms
 ✓ performMatrixRequest > uses the matrix-specific idle-timeout error for stalled raw downloads 14ms
 ✓ performMatrixRequest > uses undici runtime fetch for pinned Matrix requests so the dispatcher stays bound 4ms
 ✓ performMatrixRequest > rejects oversized JSON responses via content-length before buffering the body 3ms
 ✓ performMatrixRequest > applies streaming byte limits when JSON responses omit content-length 3ms
 ✓ performMatrixRequest > uses the JSON-specific idle-timeout error for stalled JSON downloads 3ms
 ✓ performMatrixRequest > rejects oversized raw responses when maxBytes is not provided (default MATRIX_SDK_RESPONSE_MAX_BYTES) 2ms
 ✓ performMatrixRequest > returns raw buffer bodies that stay under the default MATRIX_SDK_RESPONSE_MAX_BYTES limit 4ms
 ✓ performMatrixRequest > real HTTP server: rejects with MatrixMediaSizeLimitError when server declares over-cap Content-Length and maxBytes is omitted 68ms
 ✓ performMatrixRequest > real HTTP server: returns raw Buffer when server response is under the default cap and maxBytes is omitted 12ms
 ✓ performMatrixRequest > returns full JSON bodies that stay under the byte limit 3ms
 ✓ createMatrixGuardedFetch > rejects and cancels SDK responses above the declared size limit 3ms
 ✓ createMatrixGuardedFetch > strips matrix-js-sdk state_after sync opt-in from /sync requests 5ms
 ✓ createMatrixGuardedFetch > leaves non-sync Matrix requests unchanged 2ms

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  16 passed (16)
```

The two **real HTTP server** tests (lines 11–12 above) use a real `node:http` server bound to `127.0.0.1` with no `stubRuntimeFetch` override — the transport goes through the real undici Agent + SSRF dispatcher path end-to-end:

- **Over-cap (68 ms):** server declares `Content-Length: 67108865` (`MATRIX_SDK_RESPONSE_MAX_BYTES + 1`) with `maxBytes` omitted → `enforceDeclaredResponseSize` fires → `MatrixMediaSizeLimitError` thrown before any body bytes are buffered.
- **Under-cap (12 ms):** server sends 31-byte payload with `maxBytes` omitted → transport applies the default cap, body passes through → `Buffer` returned correctly.

## Security & Privacy

No new network destinations. No credential handling. Narrows a memory-exhaustion path — responses that would previously buffer without limit now reject with `MatrixMediaSizeLimitError` at 64 MiB.

## Compatibility

| Scenario | Before | After |
|---|---|---|
| `downloadContent(url)` with large media | OOM / process crash | Fail-closed `MatrixMediaSizeLimitError` |
| `downloadContent(url)` with normal media | Returns full Buffer | Returns full Buffer (unchanged) |
| Caller provides explicit `maxBytes` | Bounded | Bounded (unchanged) |
target.author: alix-007

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
